name: Glass-Laminate Potato Pavé with Truffle Aerated Emulsion
description: A structural culinary masterpiece featuring 50+ hyper-precise layers of Yukon Gold potatoes, compressed and slow-cooked to a velvet interior with a glass-like crisp exterior, accompanied by a light-as-air truffle-infused warm aeration.
keywords: potato pavé, michelin style, truffle aeration, molecular gastronomy, haute cuisine

steps.instruction: Peel and square potatoes into uniform blocks. Using a Japanese Vegetable Sheeter, create continuous ribbons 0.5mm thick. Brush each ribbon with clarified butter, sea salt, and micro-planed thyme.

Line a terrine mold with parchment. Layer the ribbons precisely, folding back and forth to reach at least 50 layers. Place the mold in a vacuum bag and process through a Chamber Vacuum Sealer on maximum pressure to remove all oxygen and bond layers.
Cook the sealed terrine in a sous-vide water bath at 84°C for 2 hours. Immediately transfer to an ice bath, then refrigerate under a 2kg weight for 12 hours to set the starch structure.

Prepare the aeration: Simmer reserved potato trimmings in milk and cream until tender. Blend with truffle paste and oil until homogenous. Pass through a fine-mesh chinois into an ISI Gourmet Whip. Charge with two N2O canisters and hold in a 60°C water bath.
Portioning: Remove the cold potato block and slice into perfect 2-inch cubes. Sear two sides in a carbon steel pan with clarified butter until mahogany and crisp.
Plating: Place the pavé cube centrally. Dispense a precise cloud of truffle aeration to the side. Garnish with a singular flake of sea salt and optional micro-thyme.

winePairings.name: Vintage Champagne
winePairings.notes: The high acidity and brioche notes cut through the rich butter layers and highlight the truffle aroma.

winePairings.name: Meursault (White Burgundy)
winePairings.notes: A rich, buttery Chardonnay that mirrors the texture of the pavé and earthy truffle notes.
